frameworks.name: somatic marker hypothesis
frameworks.one_line: emotion-based bodily signals guide advantageous choices under uncertainty

frameworks.name: event-related potentials of decision making
frameworks.one_line: ERP components index option processing, response anticipation, and feedback evaluation

frameworks.name: emotional valence lateralization
frameworks.one_line: positive and negative affect engage left and right hemispheres asymmetrically

core_question: What are the ERP signatures of decision making under uncertainty across option processing, response, and feedback stages in the Iowa Gambling Task?
key_assumptions: IGT validly probes decision making under uncertain reward structure
ERP components reflect stage-specific cognitive and affective processes
hemispheric lateralization indexes valence of emotional processing
manipulations: response type ('turn over' vs 'not turn over')
key_findings: advantage and disadvantage decks evoked similar P3 and late negative ERP modulations during option processing
P3 amplitude lateralized: advantage decks larger on left, disadvantage decks larger on right
'not turn over' responses preceded by larger negative ERP modulation (anticipatory effect)
P200, FRN, and P300 sensitive to feedback valence; FRN insensitive to amount
interpretations: ERP timing provides electrophysiological evidence for the somatic marker hypothesis
hemispheric P3 asymmetry reflects positive/negative emotion lateralization during uncertain choice
